title: penstemon tolmiei subsp. formosus
intro: Penstemon tolmiei subsp. formosus, also known as the Sierra beardtongue or Formosan penstemon, is a rare and unique perennial plant native to the western United States, particularly in California, and parts of Mexico. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Penstemon tolmiei subsp. formosus are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions, teas, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits, although caution and proper preparation are necessary due to the plant's potential toxicity.
